title: Replace all PyDict_GetItem with exception-safe alternatives
author: jensens
body:
## Summary

- Replace all remaining `PyDict_GetItem()` calls in the C extension with
  exception-safe alternatives (`PyDict_Contains`, `PyDict_GetItemWithError`)
- `PyDict_GetItem` silently swallows ALL exceptions from `__hash__`/`__eq__`,
  causing `isOrExtends()` to return `False` instead of raising `TypeError`
  for unhashable objects (CPython 3.14 warns about this at runtime)
- Use `PyType_GetDict()` on Python 3.13+ instead of deprecated `tp_dict`
  for free-threading safety

## Changes

| Site | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| `SB_extends` (line 335) | `PyDict_GetItem` | `PyDict_Contains` |
| `IB__adapt__` (line 795) | `PyDict_GetItem` | `PyDict_Contains` |
| `IB__call__` (line 906) | `PyDict_GetItem` on `tp_dict` | `PyDict_Contains` + `PyType_GetDict()` on 3.13+ |
| Polyfill `_PyDict_GetItemRef` | `PyDict_GetItem` | `PyDict_GetItemWithError` |

## Performance impact

`PyDict_Contains` is **faster** than `PyDict_GetItem`: it skips the
`PyErr_Fetch`/`PyErr_Restore` error-state save/restore that `PyDict_GetItem`
does on every call (~12 memory operations saved per call on `SB_extends`,
the hottest function in the extension).

## Test plan

- [x] New tests: `test_isOrExtends_raises_TypeError_for_unhashable` (C + Py)
- [x] New tests: `test_isOrExtends_propagates_MemoryError_from_hash` (C + Py)
- [x] Full test suite passes: 1366 tests, both `PURE_PYTHON=0` and `PURE_PYTHON=1`
- [x] No regressions

Fixes #357

Based on analysis from https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/5afbad864934b165a2cc080f110aa720
